You are a programming expert. 
You will be provided with a patch used to fix a bug. 

Your task is summarize the modifications made in the patch.
Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From 2412c90ba5584fed123fa6a33e752e6c8eaf74e9 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Oliver Heger <oheger@apache.org>
Date: Sat, 24 May 2014 20:11:58 +0000
Subject: [PATCH] [BEANUTILS-463] Added an easy way to suppress the class
 property.

A specialized instance suppressing the class property common to all Java
objects has been added as constant to SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector. A
test case was created demonstrating that the property can no longer be accessed
with this introspector being active.

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/commons/proper/beanutils/trunk@1597344 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/beanutils/SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java b/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/beanutils/SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java
index 450c1e08..9d4e3b30 100644
--- a/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/beanutils/SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java
+++ b/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/beanutils/SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java
@@ -21,6 +21,17 @@ import java.util.Set;
  * @since 1.9.2
  */
 public class SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector implements BeanIntrospector {
+    /**
+     * A specialized instance which is configured to suppress the special {@code class}
+     * properties of Java beans. Unintended access to the property {@code class} (which is
+     * common to all Java objects) can be a security risk because it also allows access to
+     * the class loader. Adding this instance as {@code BeanIntrospector} to an instance
+     * of {@code PropertyUtilsBean} suppresses the {@code class} property; it can then no
+     * longer be accessed.
+     */
+    public static final SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector SUPPRESS_CLASS =
+            new SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector(Collections.singleton("class"));
+
     /** A set with the names of the properties to be suppressed. */
     private final Set<String> propertyNames;

### Modifications in Each File

1. **SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java**
   - A new constant `SUPPRESS_CLASS` has been added, which is an instance of `SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector` configured to suppress the `class` property common to all Java objects.
   - A detailed comment has been included to explain the purpose of this constant, highlighting the security risks associated with accessing the `class` property and how this instance can be used to prevent such access.

### Relationships
The modification in `SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java` introduces a new feature that enhances the security of Java beans by preventing access to the `class` property. This change is significant as it provides a straightforward way to suppress a property that could lead to security vulnerabilities, thereby improving the overall robustness of the `BeanIntrospector` functionality. The addition of the test case mentioned in the patch description would likely validate this new behavior, ensuring that the `class` property is indeed inaccessible when the `SUPPRESS_CLASS` instance is used.

Based on your summary and the commit message in the patch, you need to:

1. analyze the root cause of the bug in detail. 
2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug. 



Provide a response strictly in the following format:
Root cause:<Root cause>
file:<file name>

2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug. 



Provide a response strictly in the following format:
Root cause:<Root cause>
file:<file name>

You are a programming expert. 
You will be provided with a patch used to fix a bug. 

Your task is summarize the modifications made in the patch.
Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/beanutils/SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java b/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/beanutils/SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java
index 450c1e08..9d4e3b30 100644
--- a/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/beanutils/SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java
+++ b/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/beanutils/SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java
@@ -21,6 +21,17 @@ import java.util.Set;
  * @since 1.9.2
  */
 public class SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector implements BeanIntrospector {
+    /**
+     * A specialized instance which is configured to suppress the special {@code class}
+     * properties of Java beans. Unintended access to the property {@code class} (which is
+     * common to all Java objects) can be a security risk because it also allows access to
+     * the class loader. Adding this instance as {@code BeanIntrospector} to an instance
+     * of {@code PropertyUtilsBean} suppresses the {@code class} property; it can then no
+     * longer be accessed.
+     */
+    public static final SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector SUPPRESS_CLASS =
+            new SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector(Collections.singleton("class"));
+
     /** A set with the names of the properties to be suppressed. */
     private final Set<String> propertyNames;

### Modifications in Each File

1. **SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java**
   - A new constant `SUPPRESS_CLASS` has been added, which is an instance of `SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector` configured to suppress the `class` property common to all Java objects. 
   - A comment has been added to explain the purpose of this constant, highlighting that it prevents unintended access to the `class` property, which can pose a security risk.

### Relationships
The modification in `SuppressPropertiesBeanIntrospector.java` introduces a new feature that enhances the security of Java beans by allowing the suppression of the `class` property. This change is intended to be used in conjunction with instances of `PropertyUtilsBean`, where the new constant can be applied to prevent access to the `class` property, thereby mitigating potential security vulnerabilities associated with it.

[BEANUTILS-463] Added an easy way to suppress the class

The root cause of the bug is the unintended access to the `class` property of Java objects, which poses a security risk as it allows access to the class loader and other sensitive information. This can lead to potential vulnerabilities in applications that utilize Java bean introspection without proper safeguards in place.
